---
title: "Meeting Minutes"
toc: true
number-sections: false
---

# Project Meeting 1

**Date:** 6 June 2026

**Time:** 4:00 to 5:30 PM

**In Attendance:** Chen ZeXin, Meng Tingyu, Meng Xinyu

### Agenda Items

1. Review each member's Take-home Exercise 2 and compare findings
2. Decide on the project's Mini-Challenge
3. Set up the shared project repository and website
4. Agree on the team working method
5. Draft the project proposal
6. Action items

### Agenda Item 1: Review of Take-home Exercise 2

Before the meeting, each of us had finished our own Take-home Exercise 2 on the
TenantThread embargo breach, and we had shared our work so the team could read it
in advance. During the meeting we went through all three reports together and
compared what we found.

- All three of us independently reached the same core reading of the case. The
  breach was neither a clean deliberate leak nor a simple technical failure. It
  was closer to the embargo being reasoned past under pressure.

- All three identified the same decision core, the Legal, Platform-Trust, and
  Social-Manager agents, with PR and the interns on the edges.

- All three found the shift into hidden and unattributable channels, with
  anonymous posting appearing for the first time on the crisis day.

- All three flagged the 29 May near miss as an early warning that was noticed and
  then waved through, and all three pointed to the Judge as a monitor that could
  warn but not block.

- We noted where our approaches differed, since that is where the strongest
  material for the group app sits. One of us leaned on a clear inside versus
  outside timeline and a disciplined colour palette. One built the most compact
  data spine and a searchable table of off pattern actions. One went deepest on
  data cleaning, on the shift in the agents' private reasoning over time, and on
  the language of justification.

### Agenda Item 2: Decision on the Mini-Challenge

- We agreed to build the project on VAST Challenge 2026 Mini-Challenge 1, the
  embargo breach.

- The main reason is that all three of us already did our Take-home Exercise 2 on
  this challenge, so we share a strong head start on the data and the analysis.

### Agenda Item 3: Repository and website setup

- We set up one shared GitHub repository for the project so that all three of us
  work into the same site rather than separate ones.

- All three members were added as collaborators with push access. We confirmed
  everyone could see the repository and the contributor list after a first change
  was committed.

- The repository was cloned locally and a Quarto site was scaffolded with the
  standard pages, including the proposal, methodology, findings, poster, meeting
  minutes, user guide, and about pages.

- The site was rendered locally to confirm it built, then pushed to GitHub and
  connected to a new Vercel deployment so the team has a live shared site.

### Agenda Item 4: Team working method

- Because three of us will push to the same repository, we discussed how to avoid
  file conflicts.

- We agreed that each person owns their own files where possible, so we are rarely
  editing the same file at the same time.

- We agreed on the habit of pulling the latest changes before starting work and
  pushing again when finished, and on committing in small, frequent steps rather
  than saving up large changes.

### Agenda Item 5: Project proposal

- We agreed the proposal should be a synthesis of our three Take-home Exercises
  rather than one person's work, in line with the course requirement.

- We discussed the strongest pieces from each report and how they could fit
  together into a single interactive application, organised around the
  investigative questions of the challenge.

- We drafted a first version of the proposal covering the motivation, the problem
  statement and objectives, the data, the methodology, a prototype storyboard of
  the planned app modules, the R packages, and the project schedule.

- We noted that the proposal is due on 10 June and agreed to circulate the draft
  for everyone to review and adjust before it is finalised.

### Agenda Item 6: Action Items

| Action | Owner | Due |
|---|---|---|
| Review the proposal draft and suggest changes | All members | Before 10 June |
| Confirm the dataset file name used across the project | All members | Next meeting |
| Prepare hand drawn sketches of the four app modules | All members | Next meeting |
| Add the three member names to the proposal and about page | All members | Before 10 June |
| Finalise and publish the proposal on the project site | All members | By 10 June |
| Start the methodology pages, one per member | All members | Next meeting |
